title: Conjecture 1
context_text: The paper proves $m(n)=\Theta(n^{3/2}/\ln n)$ with matching constants $4/3$ (lower) and $5/3$ (upper) in Theorem 1. The authors state that closing this gap and pinning down the exact constant requires additional ideas, and suspect $4/3$ is correct.

title: Conjecture 2
context_text: Theorem 2 establishes $M(n)=\Theta(n^3/\ln n)$ with lower constant $1/6$ and upper constant approximately $0.526$. The authors suspect $1/6$ is the correct multiplicative factor for the fixed-size variant $M_n(n)$.

title: Conjecture 3
context_text: The authors describe these prime-restricted conjectures as 'apparently much weaker' than Conjectures 1–2, viewing understanding primes as a key step towards solving more general settings. Here $\mathbb{P}(x)$ denotes the set of primes not exceeding $x$.

title: Conjecture 4
context_text: Closely related to Conjecture 3. The lower bounds in Conjectures 3 and 4 follow from the proofs of the lower bounds in Theorems 1 and 5, respectively. The upper bounds remain open.

title: Conjecture 5
statement_text: For every $k$ satisfying $\Omega(\sqrt{n})=k<\sqrt{n}$, we have [asymptotic formula for $m_k(n)$ — mathematical conclusion truncated in source].
context_text: If Conjecture 1 holds, it also resolves $m_k(n)$ asymptotically for $k\geqslant\sqrt{n}$ via Theorem 4. The only remaining open case is $k<\sqrt{n}$ with $k\not=o(\sqrt{n})$, which this conjecture addresses.
notes: The mathematical conclusion after 'we have' is truncated in the extracted source; only the quantifier range is visible.

title: Conjecture 6
statement_text: If $k\ll n$, then [formula for $M_k(n)$], while otherwise, [formula for $M_k(n)$] — mathematical conclusions truncated in source.
context_text: Conjectured asymptotic behaviour of $M_k(n)$ for $k<n$ in view of Theorem 5, splitting into two regimes according to whether $k\ll n$ or not.
notes: The mathematical formulas in both cases are truncated in the extracted source; only the conditional structure is visible.

title: Conjecture 7
statement_text: For every fixed $n$ and $k$ tending to infinity, we have [asymptotic formula — mathematical conclusion truncated in source].
context_text: Concerns packing of $k$ arithmetic progressions of the form $B_d=\{d,2d,\ldots,nd\}$ for $d=1,2,\ldots,k$ with $n$ fixed. The authors suspect the trivial lower bound of $nk$ is asymptotically correct as $k\to\infty$.
notes: The mathematical conclusion after 'we have' is truncated in the extracted source; context strongly suggests the conjecture is $M_k(n)\sim nk$ as $k\to\infty$ for fixed $n$.
